screen.extra_items.technical_book.left_p: Euler's formula e^(ix) = cos(x) + i sin(x) is a fundamental identity linking complex exponentials and trigonometric functions.
screen.extra_items.technical_book.right_: When x = π, we have cos(π) = -1 and sin(π) = 0, so the expression simplifies to e^(iπ) = -1.
screen.extra_items.technical_book.explan: Substituting x = π into Euler's formula e^(ix) = cos(x) + i sin(x) gives e^(iπ) = -1. Therefore, e^(iπ) + 1 = 0.
screen.extra_items.technical_book.footno: It is known as a beautiful identity because it links e, i, π, 1, and 0 in a single equation.
